threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver doesn't wait for you to sit down before taking off. Classic newbie move.
Caught the 59 from Brixton to Croydon last night; the driver was blasting 90s R&B. Seriously, the vibe was better than a club!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a guy casually duct-taping his bike back together at the corner bakery while ordering a croissant.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find guys selling vinyl records out of a bicycle cart, stopping traffic just to spin some tunes. Pure Berlin vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they can name the last time the BART had a full week without a delay. That’s a rare breed now.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy playing a saxophone on the Muni bus—totally off key but somehow perfect for the chaos. Only in the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's plazas are for lounging. In reality, they're often packed for local festivals, and they miss the real vibe when they just pass through.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot them by their hesitant pause at crosswalks—still learning that the green man doesn’t always mean go in this city.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still take the bus to get to the station instead of just walking five minutes to the tube. Proper locals don’t bother with the bus unless it’s raining.
Caught the N1 last week; a couple argued about who lost the last kebab, while the driver sighed like he’d seen it all before. Classic late night vibes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy wearing lederhosen at the bakery, casually picking up a croissant like it was the most normal thing.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can a kebab shop serve vegan döner at 3 AM while the street DJ mixes techno with '90s pop hits. Pure Berlin vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate around the BART delays; they know to avoid Civic Center without breaking a sweat—or even look at a schedule.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy in a tuxedo arguing with a pigeon at the Civic Center station. Must’ve been a tough night for someone.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always think we spend siesta time napping, but the real locals are out gardening or fixing up the front of their pisos.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at the unpredictable train schedules. Only locals seem unfazed by the chaos.
